items.title: Prove reproducibility
items.prefer: Restart & Run All before sharing results.
items.example: Before pushing a notebook to git or sending to a teammate: Kernel → Restart & Run All. If anything fails, fix it; only then commit/share.

items.title: Set up once
items.prefer: Initialize expensive objects in one early cell.
items.why: Leverages the persistent kernel; saves time/cost.
items.example:
```python
# Cell 2 (once)
llm = OpenAI(); enc = tiktoken.get_encoding('o200k_base')
# Cells 3..50 (instant)
resp = llm.chat.completions.create(...)
```

items.title: Keep cells small and ordered
items.avoid: Giant cells doing many unrelated things.
items.prefer: Focused cells in logical top-to-bottom order.
items.why: Easier to re-run, read and debug.
items.example: Split a 200-line 'do everything' cell into: imports → load data → preprocess → train → evaluate → visualize. Re-running the failing step now takes seconds, not minutes.

items.title: Mind the kernel state
items.avoid: Relying on variables whose defining cell you edited/deleted.
items.prefer: Re-run dependents after edits; check with %whos.
items.example: `%whos` lists every variable currently in the kernel — handy for spotting leftovers from earlier experiments. After major edits, prefer Kernel → Restart & Run All over hoping you re-ran the right cells.
